# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    name: Build and Cache Dependencies
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: 14

      - name: Cache node_modules
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Install Node.js dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Cache pip packages and config files
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip, ./*  # Adjust paths as needed
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/pyproject.toml', '**/setup.cfg') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install pip dependencies
        run: pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
        env:
          PYTHONUNBUFFERED: 1

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Cache environment-specific configs
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: .env  # Adjust path as needed
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-env-${{ github.ref }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-env-
